chapter_title.ar: باب الْيَمِينِ الَّتِي تَلْزَمُ صَاحِبَهَا الْكَفَّارَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 12 – The oath which necessitates the expiation upon its swearer

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/325)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/325)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَمَّا يُكَفَّرُ مِنَ الْأَيْمَانِ فَقَالَ مَا كَانَ عَلَيْكَ أَنْ تَفْعَلَهُ فَحَلَفْتَ أَنْ لَا تَفْعَلَهُ فَفَعَلْتَهُ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ شَيْ‏ءٌ إِذَا فَعَلْتَهُ وَ مَا لَمْ يَكُنْ عَلَيْكَ وَاجِباً أَنْ تَفْعَلَهُ فَحَلَفْتَ أَنْ لَا تَفْعَلَهُ ثُمَّ فَعَلْتَهُ فَعَلَيْكَ الْكَفَّارَةُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from hi sfather, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Jameel Bin Darraj, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5<sup>th</sup> or 6<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>), said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about what would (necessitate) an expiation from the oaths. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Whatever (was Obligatory) upon you to do it, so you swear that you would not do it, then you do it, so there is nothing upon you when you do it; and whatever does not happen to be Obligatory upon you to do it, so you swear that you would not be doing it, then you do it, so upon you is the expiation’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Jamil ibn Darraj from Zurarah who has narrated the following: “I once asked one of the two Imam, (abu Ja‘far or abu ‘Abd Allah), ‘Alayhim al-Salam, about the case of the kind of oath which requires one to pay an expiation. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is when you are obligated to do something and you take an oath not to do it but you do it then there is nothing on you when you do it. What is not obligatory on you to do and you take an oath not to do it then you do it, then there is expiation on you.’”
